ddls:
CREATE TABLE "generators" (
  "bus" INTEGER /* Bus number the generator is connected to */,
  "Pg" REAL /* Actual real power output of the generator */,
  "Qg" REAL /* Actual reactive power output of the generator */,
  "Qmax" REAL /* Maximum reactive power output of the generator */,
  "Qmin" REAL /* Minimum reactive power output of the generator */,
  "Vg" REAL /* Voltage level at the generator bus */,
  "mBase" REAL /* Total MVA base of the generator */,
  "status" INTEGER /* Status of the generator (0 for off, 1 for on) */,
  "Pmax" REAL /* Maximum real power output of the generator */,
  "Pmin" REAL /* Minimum real power output of the generator */,
  "efficiency" REAL /* Efficiency of the generator */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the next scheduled maintenance */,
  "operating_hours" INTEGER /* Total operating hours of the generator */,
  "fuel_type" TEXT /* Type of fuel used by the generator */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the generator */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("bus"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_generators_status FOREIGN KEY ("status") REFERENCES generator_status ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "buses" (
  "bus_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each bus */,
  "bus_name" TEXT /* Name of the bus */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the bus */,
  "voltage_level" REAL /* Voltage level at the bus */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographical latitude of the bus */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographical longitude of the bus */,
  "capacity" REAL /* Maximum capacity of the bus in terms of power */,
  "connected_generators" INTEGER /* Number of generators connected to the bus */,
  "last_inspection_date" TEXT /* Date of the last inspection of the bus */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("bus_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "generator_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_description" TEXT /* Description of the generator status */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the status */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who last updated the status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_generator_status_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_records" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "bus" INTEGER /* Bus number the generator is connected to */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of maintenance */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance performed */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance work */,
  "technician_id" INTEGER /* ID of the technician who performed the maintenance */,
  "next_scheduled_date" TEXT /* Date of the next scheduled maintenance */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost of the maintenance work */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_bus FOREIGN KEY ("bus") REFERENCES buses ("bus_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_technician_id FOREIGN KEY ("technician_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, dispatcher, analyst) */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "created_on" TEXT /* Date when the user account was created */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
